etymology.description: 白(白) (bái, "white") + phonetic 早(早) (zǎo) -> acorn; used phonetically for 皂(皂) (zào, "black")
etymology.interpretation: 皂(皂) (zào, "acorn") uses 早(早) (zǎo) for its sound, with 白(白) (bái, "white") as the semantic component originally coupled to mean acorn. Over time, its meaning extended to black due to phonetic borrowing, showing an intriguing shift contrasting its visual elements.

examples.chinese: 我第一次看到皂(皂)是在森林里(在森林裡)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ dì yī cì kàn dào zào shì zài sēnlín lǐ.
examples.english: I saw acorns for the first time in the forest.

examples.chinese: 秋天时(秋天時)，树林里到处都是皂(樹林裡到處都是皂)。
examples.pinyin: Qiūtiān shí, shùlín lǐ dàochù dōu shì zào.
examples.english: In autumn, acorns are everywhere in the woods.

examples.chinese: 松鼠会储存皂(松鼠會儲存皂)，以备冬天(以備冬天)。
examples.pinyin: Sōngshǔ huì chǔcún zào, yǐ bèi dōngtiān.
examples.english: Squirrels will store acorns to prepare for winter.
